objective: Architectural sign-off on grove doctor: core::harness sentinel constants, pure read-only core::doctor::diagnose, and the additive Cmd::Doctor CLI verb with --json and exit-code semantics.
key_changes: Constant ownership resolved cleanly: core/src/harness.rs is the single source of truth; init.rs imports via grove_core::harness — no core->cli cycle, no duplication.
core::doctor::diagnose is pure and read-only, composing existing core primitives (active_mode, verify_lock, ExploreConfig::validate, health probes) rather than reimplementing them.
Reuse over reinvention: lock_integrity delegates to T06 verify_lock; explore checks gate on Mode::McpLlm and surface HealthError hints verbatim — no shadow logic that could drift.
Exit-code + --json dual-format keyed off Report::ok() (all !Fail) gives a stable CI-consumable contract; warn-only-exits-zero boundary explicitly tested.
Independent code review isolated true T07 footprint via git diff HEAD (vs committed T04 reconcile_harness); clippy -D warnings clean; 303 tests green, 0 failed.
Validation independently confirmed all 10 ACs pass.
Deployment: version bump required at release (new user-facing verb); no regeneration, no migration, no security scan — purely additive and read-only, zero risk to existing deployments.
Follow-up (non-blocking): echo effective/forced mode in human header when --explore/--standard override declared mode.
Follow-up (non-blocking): dedicated named steering-legality check for traceability; currently transitive via ExploreConfig::validate().
Follow-up (cosmetic): remove double blank line left in init.rs after MCP_SERVER_KEY const migration.
